counterfactual_mining.design: shared-board paired (skip, debate) outcomes per question; scripts/gen_action_wm_dataset.py
counterfactual_mining.headline: debate helps 27/592 (4.6%), hurts 0/592; p(correct|debate) ~= 0.995-1.0

action_wm_training.auroc_heldout_note: honestly null — heldout debate_correct is degenerate all-ones
action_wm_training.platt_note: class-rebalanced head is level-biased (mean 0.78 on all-correct band); Platt (a=2.04,b=5.43) fit on train band ships in the artifact — without it PlannedGate degenerates to never-fire

end_to_end_eval.regime: bench.session steps=8, default rumor 0.35, unlimited debates unless noted

board_error_repair.note: both learned gates fire on and correct every question the ungated board misses (3/3)

end_to_end_eval.planned_vs_reactive_verdict: No accuracy separation in any tested regime (both 100%); the reactive threshold gate is ~2x more debate-frugal (7.8% vs 14.1% fire rate, 731 vs 1069 tokens/q). Planning's argmax overfires because calibrated p(correct|debate)~=1 makes predicted uplift positive almost everywhere while lambda*cost is small. The WM's measured value is economy vs always-debate: same 100% accuracy at ~1/14 the cost ($0.50 vs $7.09 per 100 questions).

live_maintain.note: Live implementation of the imagination-audited winning policy: one real skeptic->judge debate per evidence batch on the learned-risk top key; questions serve instantly from the board (gate_mode=never). Trade: ~2x reactive-gate cost for zero serving-path debate latency.
